test_name: Create product config draft — full valid SBL payload
preconditions: No existing product_configs row for product_code='SBL' in the org
test_steps: Body: { product_code: 'SBL', name: 'Secured Business Loan v1', field_schema: { groups: [{ id: 'g1', label: 'Business Info', fields: [{ id: 'constitution', label: 'Constitution', type: 'select', mandatory: true, options: ['pvt_ltd','partnership','proprietorship'] }, { id: 'turnover', label: 'Annual Turnover', type: 'number', mandatory: true }, { id: 'gstin', label: 'GSTIN', type: 'text', mandatory: false }] }] }, document_checklist: { items: [{ doc_type: 'gst', label: 'GST Certificate', mandatory: true }, { doc_type: 'itr', label: 'ITR 3 years', mandatory: true }] }, sla_config: { first_contact_hours: 4, kyc_days: 3 }, eligibility_mapping: { fields: [{ lms_field: 'turnover', los_field: 'annualTurnover' }, { lms_field: 'constitution', los_field: 'entityType' }] }, pan_required_at: 'at_capture' }
Assert response envelope: data.status = 'draft', data.config_version_status = 'pending', data.version = 1
Query DB: SELECT status, version, org_id, created_by FROM product_configs WHERE product_code='SBL'
Query DB: SELECT status, maker_id, config_type FROM configuration_versions WHERE config_ref = data.product_config_id
Query DB: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM event_outbox WHERE event_code='CONFIG_CHANGED'
expected_result: HTTP 201. data.status='draft', data.config_version_status='pending', data.version=1. DB: product_configs row exists with status='draft', version=1. DB: configuration_versions row exists with status='pending', maker_id='user-admin-a', config_type='product_config'. DB: event_outbox has CONFIG_CHANGED row. Response envelope shape: { data: {...}, meta: {...}, error: null }.
postconditions: No active product config for SBL exists yet

test_name: Full maker-checker approval cycle — draft activates, previous version retires
preconditions: Seed: active product_configs row for product_code='CV', version=2, product_config_id='pc-cv-v2'
ADMIN user-A (maker) and ADMIN user-B (checker) both seeded
test_steps: As user-A: POST /api/v1/admin/products with body { product_code: 'CV', name: 'CV Config v3', field_schema: { groups: [{ id: 'g1', label: 'Asset', fields: [{ id: 'vehicle_type', label: 'Vehicle Type', type: 'select', mandatory: true, options: ['LCV','HCV','MCV'] }] }] }, document_checklist: { items: [{ doc_type: 'permit', label: 'Route Permit', mandatory: true }] }, pan_required_at: 'before_kyc' }
As user-B: POST /api/v1/admin/config/{configuration_version_id}/approve with body { action: 'approved', remarks: 'Schema validated OK' }
Query DB: SELECT status FROM product_configs WHERE product_config_id = new_pc_id
Query DB: SELECT status FROM product_configs WHERE product_config_id = 'pc-cv-v2'
Query DB: SELECT status, checker_id, effective_at FROM configuration_versions WHERE configuration_version_id = cv_id
Query DB: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM event_outbox WHERE event_code='CONFIG_CHANGED' AND payload->>'status'='active'
expected_result: HTTP 200 on approve. data.status='active', data.product_config_status='active'. DB: new product_configs row has status='active'. DB: old product_configs row 'pc-cv-v2' now has status='retired'. DB: configuration_versions row has status='active', checker_id='user-admin-b', effective_at NOT NULL. DB: event_outbox has CONFIG_CHANGED with status='active'.
postconditions: Only one active product_configs row exists for CV
Previous version 'pc-cv-v2' has status='retired' but still exists (FK integrity for in-flight leads)

test_name: Self-approval blocked — maker cannot approve own pending CV
preconditions: Seed: pending configuration_versions row created by user-A, status='pending', maker_id='user-admin-a'
test_steps: As user-A: POST /api/v1/admin/config/{cv_id}/approve with body { action: 'approved', remarks: 'Self approve' }
Query DB: SELECT status FROM configuration_versions WHERE configuration_version_id = cv_id
expected_result: HTTP 403. error.code='FORBIDDEN'. DB: configuration_versions.status remains 'pending'. No product_configs row modified.

test_name: Eligibility-mapping change blocked for BM checker (requires scope A)
preconditions: ADMIN user-A creates a draft config that modifies eligibility_mapping
test_steps: As user-A (ADMIN): POST /api/v1/admin/products with eligibility_mapping change
As user-B (BM): POST /api/v1/admin/config/{cv_id}/approve with { action: 'approved', remarks: 'OK' }
Query DB: SELECT status FROM configuration_versions WHERE configuration_version_id = cv_id
expected_result: HTTP 403. error.code='FORBIDDEN'. configuration_versions.status remains 'pending'. No product_configs activated.

test_name: Edit active config creates new draft — original row immutable
preconditions: Seed: active product_configs row for product_code='HRM', version=3, product_config_id='pc-hrm-v3', name='HRM Config v3'
test_steps: As user-A: PATCH /api/v1/admin/products/pc-hrm-v3 with body { name: 'HRM Config v3 Revised' }
Query DB: SELECT version, status, name FROM product_configs WHERE product_config_id = 'pc-hrm-v3'
Query DB: SELECT product_config_id, version, status, name FROM product_configs WHERE product_code='HRM' AND version=4
Query DB: SELECT status, maker_id FROM configuration_versions WHERE config_ref = new_pc_id
expected_result: HTTP 200. data.version=4, data.status='draft'. DB: original row 'pc-hrm-v3' still has version=3, status='active', name='HRM Config v3' (unchanged). DB: new row exists with version=4, status='draft', name='HRM Config v3 Revised'. DB: configuration_versions row with status='pending' points to new row.
postconditions: Original active config row is unchanged
New draft row exists at version 4

test_name: Edit draft config returns CONFLICT
preconditions: Seed: draft product_configs row for product_code='TW', version=1, product_config_id='pc-tw-draft'
test_steps: As user-A: PATCH /api/v1/admin/products/pc-tw-draft with body { name: 'Any Name' }
Query DB: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM product_configs WHERE product_code='TW' AND version > 1
expected_result: HTTP 409. error.code='CONFLICT'. No new product_configs rows created. Draft row unchanged.
postconditions: product_configs row count for TW unchanged

test_name: Invalid product_code returns VALIDATION_ERROR
test_steps: POST /api/v1/admin/products with body { product_code: 'TRUCK', name: 'Test', field_schema: { groups: [] }, document_checklist: { items: [] }, pan_required_at: 'before_kyc' }
Assert error.fields contains an entry with field='product_code'
expected_result: HTTP 400. error.code='VALIDATION_ERROR'. error.fields[0].field='product_code'. No DB rows inserted. Response must NOT expose stack trace.

test_name: Eligibility_mapping references undeclared lms_field — VALIDATION_ERROR
test_steps: POST /api/v1/admin/products with body { product_code: 'CE', name: 'CE Config', field_schema: { groups: [{ id: 'g1', label: 'Equipment', fields: [{ id: 'equipment_type', label: 'Equipment Type', type: 'text', mandatory: true }] }] }, document_checklist: { items: [] }, eligibility_mapping: { fields: [{ lms_field: 'invoice_value', los_field: 'assetValue' }] }, pan_required_at: 'before_kyc' }
Assert error.fields contains an entry referencing 'eligibility_mapping' and the undeclared field 'invoice_value'
expected_result: HTTP 400. error.code='VALIDATION_ERROR'. error.fields[0].field references 'eligibility_mapping.fields[0].lms_field'. No DB writes occur.

test_name: Version pinning — in-flight lead keeps pinned config after activation of new version
preconditions: Seed: active product_configs row, product_code='CAR', version=2, product_config_id='pc-car-v2'
Seed: leads row with product_config_id='pc-car-v2' (in-flight lead)
ADMIN user-A (maker) and ADMIN user-B (checker) seeded
test_steps: As user-A: POST /api/v1/admin/products to create new draft for 'CAR' (version=3). Capture new product_config_id 'pc-car-v3' and cv_id
As user-B: POST /api/v1/admin/config/{cv_id}/approve with { action: 'approved', remarks: 'OK' }
Query DB: SELECT product_config_id FROM leads WHERE product_config_id = 'pc-car-v2'
Query DB: SELECT status FROM product_configs WHERE product_config_id = 'pc-car-v2'
Query DB: SELECT status FROM product_configs WHERE product_config_id = 'pc-car-v3'
expected_result: DB: leads row still has product_config_id='pc-car-v2' (unchanged). DB: product_configs 'pc-car-v2' has status='retired' but still exists. DB: product_configs 'pc-car-v3' has status='active'. No UPDATE leads statement was executed.
postconditions: In-flight lead FK 'pc-car-v2' still valid (row exists, just retired)

di_meta.setup_sql: INSERT INTO product_configs (product_config_id, org_id, product_code, name, version, status, field_schema, document_checklist, pan_required_at, created_by, updated_by) VALUES ('pc-car-v2', '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001', 'CAR', 'Car Config v2', 2, 'active', '{"groups":[]}', '{"items":[]}', 'before_kyc', 'user-admin-a', 'user-admin-a'); INSERT INTO leads (lead_id, org_id, product_code, product_config_id, stage, created_by, updated_by) VALUES ('lead-001', '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001', 'CAR', 'pc-car-v2', 'captured', 'user-admin-a', 'user-admin-a');
di_meta.invariant_query: SELECT l.lead_id FROM leads l WHERE l.lead_id='lead-001' AND l.product_config_id != 'pc-car-v2'
di_meta.cleanup: DELETE FROM leads WHERE lead_id='lead-001'; DELETE FROM product_configs WHERE product_config_id IN ('pc-car-v2','pc-car-v3');

test_name: Rejection path — remarks required, draft retired on rejection
preconditions: ADMIN user-A creates draft config for 'TRACTOR'. Capture cv_id and draft_pc_id
test_steps: As user-B: POST /api/v1/admin/config/{cv_id}/approve with { action: 'rejected' } (no remarks field)
As user-B: POST /api/v1/admin/config/{cv_id}/approve with { action: 'rejected', remarks: 'Field mapping incorrect.' }
Query DB: SELECT status FROM configuration_versions WHERE configuration_version_id = cv_id
Query DB: SELECT status FROM product_configs WHERE product_config_id = draft_pc_id
Query DB: SELECT status FROM product_configs WHERE product_config_id = 'pc-tractor-v1'
expected_result: Step 1: HTTP 400, VALIDATION_ERROR, error.fields[0].field='remarks'. Step 2: HTTP 200, data.status='rejected'. DB: configuration_versions row has status='rejected', checker_id set. DB: draft product_configs row has status='retired'. DB: original active 'pc-tractor-v1' still has status='active' (original not changed on rejection).

test_name: Retire active config — in-flight leads unaffected
preconditions: Seed: active product_configs 'pc-tw-v1' for product_code='TW', version=1
test_steps: Query DB: SELECT status FROM product_configs WHERE product_config_id='pc-tw-v1'
Query DB: SELECT product_config_id FROM leads WHERE product_config_id='pc-tw-v1'
expected_result: HTTP 200. data.status='retired'. DB: product_configs row has status='retired'. DB: leads row still references 'pc-tw-v1' (FK valid, row exists). No UPDATE leads executed.

test_name: RM cannot access /admin/products — FORBIDDEN
preconditions: RM user JWT minted (role='RM', no 'configuration' capability)
test_steps: Assert response body does NOT contain stack trace or internal paths
expected_result: HTTP 403. error.code='FORBIDDEN'. No product_config data returned. Response body shape: { data: null, meta: null, error: { code: 'FORBIDDEN', message: '...' } }.

test_name: Transaction rollback — outbox failure rolls back all DB writes atomically
preconditions: Test double: OutboxService.emit configured to throw after product_configs INSERT
test_steps: POST /api/v1/admin/products with valid SBL body (OutboxService.emit stubbed to throw)
Query DB: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM product_configs WHERE product_code='SBL' AND created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '10 seconds'
Query DB: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM configuration_versions WHERE created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '10 seconds'
Query DB: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM event_outbox WHERE event_code='CONFIG_CHANGED' AND created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '10 seconds'
expected_result: HTTP 500. error.code='INTERNAL_ERROR'. DB: no new product_configs row persists. DB: no new configuration_versions row persists. DB: no new event_outbox row persists. Full atomicity maintained.
postconditions: Database state identical to pre-test state

di_meta.setup_sql: -- No prior state needed; clean org
di_meta.action: POST /admin/products with OutboxService.emit stubbed to throw mid-transaction
di_meta.invariant_query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM product_configs WHERE product_code='SBL' AND created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '10 seconds' HAVING COUNT(*) > 0

test_name: Approve button disabled when current user is the config maker
preconditions: User-A previously created a pending configuration_versions row (maker_id = user-A's id)
Version history page loaded showing that pending CV row
test_steps: Locate the row with status='pending' and maker_id === currentUserId
Assert the 'Approve' button is disabled (aria-disabled=true or disabled attribute)
Assert a tooltip appears explaining the self-approval restriction
expected_result: Approve button is disabled (aria-disabled='true' or disabled). Tooltip text explains: 'You submitted this change; a different user must approve.'

ui_meta.visual_assert: Approve button has disabled styling (opacity reduced or greyed out)
ui_meta.a11y: aria-disabled='true' on approve button; tooltip associated via aria-describedby

test_name: Full E2E maker-checker workflow — create HRM config draft then checker activates it
preconditions: No existing HRM product_config rows for the test org
test_steps: Login as user-A. Navigate to /admin/products. Click 'New Configuration'.

Fill form: product_code='HRM', name='Home Renovation v1', add group 'Property Details' with 2 mandatory fields (property_type: select, renovation_purpose: text), add checklist item (doc_type=property), set pan_required_at='before_kyc'. Submit.
Assert toast: 'Configuration submitted for checker approval'. Assert redirect to product list or detail page. Assert status chip shows 'draft'.
Login as user-B. Navigate to version history for the new config (/admin/products/{id}/versions).
Click 'Approve'. Fill remarks: 'All fields verified'. Submit.
Assert status chip changes to 'active'. Assert version history row shows status='active', checker_id set.
expected_result: End-to-end: new product_configs row has status='active' after checker approval. Version history shows 2 rows: the activated v1 and configuration_versions entry with status='active'. StatusChip on list page shows 'active'.

ui_meta.visual_assert: StatusChip text is 'active' with green styling
ui_meta.a11y: All form inputs reachable via Tab; status chip has role='status' or descriptive aria-label

test_name: Only one active product_config per product_code per org at any time
preconditions: Multiple activation cycles run for product_code='CV' (v1, v2, v3 each activated in sequence)
test_steps: Complete 3 sequential maker-checker approval cycles for product_code='CV'
After each activation, run invariant SQL query
Assert query returns 0 rows each time
expected_result: Invariant query returns 0 rows after each activation, confirming no more than one active config per (org_id, product_code).

di_meta.setup_sql: -- Run 3 sequential activation cycles for CV via maker-checker API calls
di_meta.invariant_query: SELECT org_id, product_code, COUNT(*) AS active_count FROM product_configs WHERE status='active' GROUP BY org_id, product_code HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
di_meta.cleanup: DELETE FROM product_configs WHERE product_code='CV' AND org_id='00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001'; DELETE FROM configuration_versions WHERE config_type='product_config';

test_name: No self-approved configuration_versions — checker_id must differ from maker_id
preconditions: Self-approval attempts made (should have been blocked at API layer)
test_steps: After any approval workflow run, execute invariant SQL query
expected_result: Invariant query returns 0 rows — no configuration_versions row has checker_id = maker_id.

di_meta.setup_sql: -- State built by prior approval-cycle tests
di_meta.action: Read-only invariant check after all approval cycle tests
di_meta.invariant_query: SELECT configuration_version_id FROM configuration_versions WHERE checker_id IS NOT NULL AND checker_id = maker_id

test_name: All 7 seeded product configs present and active after Flyway V003 migration
preconditions: Testcontainers-Postgres with Flyway migrations including V003 applied
test_steps: Assert data array contains exactly 7 items
Assert each item has status='active' and version=1
expected_result: HTTP 200. data.length=7. meta.pagination.total=7. All 7 product codes present. Each has status='active', version=1. Response envelope: { data: [...], meta: { pagination: { total: 7, limit: 25 } }, error: null }.
postconditions: Seed state confirmed — downstream FR-010 capture tests can proceed

test_name: Correct pan_required_at per product — TW=before_handoff, SBL/HRM=at_capture, others=before_kyc
test_steps: From response, extract pan_required_at for each product
Assert CV, CAR, TRACTOR, CE each have pan_required_at='before_kyc'
expected_result: Each product's pan_required_at matches domain rule: TW=before_handoff, SBL=at_capture, HRM=at_capture, CV/CAR/TRACTOR/CE=before_kyc.

test_name: CV product config has correct mandatory capture fields and document checklist
test_steps: Assert field_schema contains fields: vehicle_type, fleet_size, down_payment, dealer (check mandatory=true for at least vehicle_type)
Assert data.eligibility_mapping is non-null and contains at least one mapping entry
expected_result: HTTP 200. CV product config returned with non-null field_schema (includes vehicle_type mandatory), non-null document_checklist (includes permit, insurance), non-null eligibility_mapping.

test_name: Seed idempotency — re-running V003 migration does not create duplicate rows
test_steps: Execute V003 seed INSERT statements a second time (or re-run Flyway with repeatable migration pattern)
Query DB: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM product_configs WHERE version=1 AND status='active'
Run invariant: SELECT org_id, product_code, version, COUNT(*) FROM product_configs GROUP BY org_id, product_code, version HAVING COUNT(*) > 1

test_data.migration_statement: INSERT INTO product_configs ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING

expected_result: Row count remains 7 after second run. No error thrown. Invariant query returns 0 rows (no duplicates).
postconditions: product_configs table still has exactly 7 seed rows

di_meta.setup_sql: -- V003 applied once: 7 rows in product_configs
di_meta.invariant_query: SELECT org_id, product_code, version, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM product_configs GROUP BY org_id, product_code, version HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
di_meta.cleanup: -- No cleanup; seed state retained for other tests

test_name: RM cannot access product config list — FORBIDDEN (no configuration capability)
test_steps: Assert response body does NOT include any product_config data
Assert response does NOT expose internal paths or stack traces
expected_result: HTTP 403. error.code='FORBIDDEN'. data=null. No product config details leaked.

test_name: limit > 100 returns VALIDATION_ERROR
expected_result: HTTP 400. error.code='VALIDATION_ERROR'. error.fields[0].field='limit'. Enforces global LIMIT ≤ 100 rule.

test_name: Product picker in lead capture form shows all 7 seeded products

Assert dropdown/radio list contains exactly 7 options.
Assert options include labels for: Commercial Vehicle, Car, Tractor, Construction Equipment, Two Wheeler, Secured Business, Home Renovation.
expected_result: Product picker shows exactly 7 options with correct labels. No 'No options' empty state. All options are selectable.

ui_meta.visual_assert: 7 product options visible in dropdown; no empty state component rendered
ui_meta.a11y: Combobox has aria-expanded=true when open; each option has role='option' and accessible name

test_name: All 7 seed configs have non-null field_schema, document_checklist, eligibility_mapping
test_steps: Execute invariant SQL queries against product_configs table
Assert query for null field_schema returns 0 rows
Assert query for null document_checklist returns 0 rows
Assert query for null eligibility_mapping returns 0 rows for the 7 v1 seed rows
expected_result: All 3 invariant queries return 0 rows. All 7 seeded product configs have populated JSONB fields.

di_meta.invariant_query: SELECT product_config_id FROM product_configs WHERE version=1 AND status='active' AND (field_schema IS NULL OR document_checklist IS NULL OR eligibility_mapping IS NULL)

test_name: ADMIN creates valid scheme — 201 with audit log
preconditions: No scheme with code='DEALER-TW-Q3' exists in the org
test_steps: Body: { code: 'DEALER-TW-Q3', name: 'TW Dealer Q3 2026 Offer', product_code: 'TW', subvention_flag: true, valid_from: '2026-07-01', valid_to: '2026-09-30' }
Query DB: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM audit_logs WHERE entity_type='scheme' AND action='config_change'
expected_result: HTTP 201. data.scheme_id is non-null UUID. data.code='DEALER-TW-Q3'. data.is_active=true. DB: schemes row exists with correct values. DB: audit_logs row with action='config_change', entity_type='scheme'.
postconditions: Scheme available for RM to attach to TW leads

test_name: RM attaches valid active product-matched scheme to a TW lead
preconditions: Seed: active scheme { code: 'DEALER-TW-Q3', product_code: 'TW', valid_from: '2026-07-01', valid_to: '2026-12-31', is_active: true }
Seed: lead_id='lead-tw-001' with product_code='TW' in stage='captured'
RM user JWT minted and lead is within RM's scope
test_steps: Query DB: SELECT attributes->>'scheme_code' FROM lead_product_details WHERE lead_id='lead-tw-001'
expected_result: HTTP 200. data.lead_product_detail.attributes.scheme_code='DEALER-TW-Q3'. DB: lead_product_details row has attributes->>'scheme_code'='DEALER-TW-Q3'. DB: audit_logs row with action='lead_update' and lead_id set.
postconditions: Scheme code attached to lead; will be included in LOS eligibility payload (FR-080)

test_name: Attach expired scheme returns VALIDATION_ERROR
preconditions: Seed: scheme { code: 'EXPIRED-TW-Q1', product_code: 'TW', valid_from: '2026-01-01', valid_to: '2026-03-31', is_active: true } (valid_to is yesterday relative to test date 2026-06-17)
Seed: TW lead 'lead-tw-002' in scope of RM user
test_steps: Query DB: SELECT attributes->>'scheme_code' FROM lead_product_details WHERE lead_id='lead-tw-002'
expected_result: HTTP 400. error.code='VALIDATION_ERROR'. error.fields[0].field='scheme_code'. error message mentions 'expired'. DB: lead_product_details.attributes has no scheme_code (unchanged).

test_name: Attach scheme for wrong product — VALIDATION_ERROR
preconditions: Seed: scheme { code: 'DEALER-CAR-Q3', product_code: 'CAR', valid_to: '2026-12-31', is_active: true }
test_steps: Assert error message contains 'not available for this product' (case-insensitive)
expected_result: HTTP 400. error.code='VALIDATION_ERROR'. error.fields[0].field='scheme_code'. Product mismatch error message returned.

test_name: Duplicate scheme code returns CONFLICT
preconditions: Scheme with code='DUPE-SCHEME-01' already created in the org
test_steps: First POST /api/v1/admin/schemes with { code: 'DUPE-SCHEME-01', name: 'First', product_code: 'CV', valid_from: '2026-07-01', valid_to: '2026-12-31' } — assert 201
Query DB: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM schemes WHERE code='DUPE-SCHEME-01'
expected_result: First call: HTTP 201. Second call: HTTP 409. error.code='CONFLICT'. DB: exactly 1 schemes row with code='DUPE-SCHEME-01'.

di_meta.setup_sql: -- First POST creates the scheme row

test_name: Create scheme with valid_to before valid_from — VALIDATION_ERROR

Assert error.fields contains entry with field='valid_to'
expected_result: HTTP 400. error.code='VALIDATION_ERROR'. error.fields[0].field='valid_to'. No schemes row inserted.

test_name: Scheme with valid_from = valid_to (one-day scheme) — attach succeeds on that day
preconditions: Seed: scheme { code: 'ONE-DAY-SCHEME', product_code: 'HRM', valid_from: '2026-06-17', valid_to: '2026-06-17', is_active: true } (today is 2026-06-17)
test_steps: PATCH /api/v1/leads/lead-hrm-001 with RM JWT, body { product_detail: { scheme_code: 'ONE-DAY-SCHEME' } }
Confirm: boundary condition valid_to = today is treated as NOT expired
expected_result: HTTP 200. Scheme successfully attached. valid_to = today is not expired (boundary: inclusive end date).

expected_result: HTTP 403. error.code='FORBIDDEN'. No schemes row created.

test_name: Null product_code scheme attaches to any lead product
preconditions: Seed: scheme { code: 'GLOBAL-SCHEME-01', product_code: null, valid_from: '2026-01-01', valid_to: '2099-12-31', is_active: true }
Seed: CV lead 'lead-cv-002' in RM scope

expected_result: HTTP 200. Scheme with null product_code successfully attaches to a CV lead (null = all-product scheme). Product match check passes.

test_name: Scheme attach preserves existing lead_product_detail attributes
preconditions: Seed: lead_product_details row for 'lead-car-001' with attributes = { vehicle_reg: 'MH01AB1234', down_payment: 50000 }
Seed: active scheme { code: 'DEALER-CAR-Q3', product_code: 'CAR', valid_to: '2099-12-31', is_active: true }
test_steps: PATCH /api/v1/leads/lead-car-001 with RM JWT, body { product_detail: { scheme_code: 'DEALER-CAR-Q3' } }
expected_result: HTTP 200. After PATCH, attributes JSONB contains both pre-existing keys (vehicle_reg, down_payment) and new key (scheme_code). No existing attribute keys dropped.

di_meta.setup_sql: INSERT INTO lead_product_details (lead_id, org_id, product_config_id, attributes, created_by, updated_by) VALUES ('lead-car-001', '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001', 'pc-car-v1', '{"vehicle_reg":"MH01AB1234","down_payment":50000}', 'user-rm-001', 'user-rm-001');
di_meta.invariant_query: SELECT lead_id FROM lead_product_details WHERE lead_id='lead-car-001' AND (attributes->>'vehicle_reg' IS NULL OR attributes->>'down_payment' IS NULL)
di_meta.cleanup: UPDATE lead_product_details SET attributes = '{"vehicle_reg":"MH01AB1234","down_payment":50000}'::jsonb WHERE lead_id='lead-car-001';

test_name: Unauthenticated request to create scheme returns AUTH_REQUIRED
expected_result: HTTP 401. error.code='AUTH_REQUIRED'. No scheme created.

test_name: List schemes — pagination limit enforced at 100
test_steps: Assert HTTP 200 (no 4xx for clamping) OR HTTP 400 with VALIDATION_ERROR
expected_result: If limit is clamped: HTTP 200, meta.pagination.limit=100. If limit is rejected: HTTP 400, error.code='VALIDATION_ERROR'. Either way, no more than 100 rows returned.

test_name: Scheme picker shows active product-matched schemes; expired scheme visually flagged
preconditions: Seed: active scheme for TW ('DEALER-TW-Q3', valid_to: future)
Seed: expired scheme for TW ('EXPIRED-TW-Q1', valid_to: past)
RM logged in; on lead 360 page for TW lead
test_steps: Assert 'DEALER-TW-Q3' option is visible and selectable
Assert 'EXPIRED-TW-Q1' option either does not appear or is marked aria-disabled=true with 'Expired' status chip
Reload page. Assert scheme picker shows 'DEALER-TW-Q3' as selected value
expected_result: Active scheme selectable. Expired scheme absent or aria-disabled. After selection: Toast 'Scheme attached'. After reload: scheme still attached.
postconditions: Scheme persisted on lead after page reload

ui_meta.visual_assert: Active scheme option has no disabled styling; expired scheme chip has 'Expired' label with muted/warning color
ui_meta.a11y: Expired option has aria-disabled='true'; active option is focusable; Combobox label associated via aria-labelledby

test_name: No lead_product_details references a scheme code that does not exist in schemes table
test_steps: Execute invariant SQL query joining lead_product_details.attributes->>'scheme_code' against schemes table
Assert 0 rows returned (no orphaned scheme_code references)
expected_result: 0 rows returned — every non-null scheme_code in lead_product_details.attributes references a valid schemes row.

di_meta.setup_sql: -- State built by prior scheme attach tests
di_meta.action: Read-only logical FK invariant check (no DB FK on JSONB field)
di_meta.invariant_query: SELECT lpd.lead_id, lpd.attributes->>'scheme_code' AS bad_code FROM lead_product_details lpd WHERE lpd.attributes->>'scheme_code' IS NOT NULL AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM schemes s WHERE s.code = lpd.attributes->>'scheme_code' AND s.org_id = lpd.org_id)

test_name: No duplicate (org_id, code) in schemes table
preconditions: Several scheme creation attempts including duplicate code attempts (all rejected at API layer)
test_steps: Execute uniqueness invariant SQL query on schemes table
expected_result: 0 rows — unique constraint on (org_id, code) is upheld. No duplicate scheme codes exist.

di_meta.setup_sql: -- State built by prior scheme tests including CONFLICT-rejected duplicates
di_meta.invariant_query: SELECT org_id, code, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM schemes GROUP BY org_id, code HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
